records.specific_details: Seeking Government measures to support the continued recovery of Ireland's tourism and hospitality industry
records.intended_results: Restoration of the 9% rate of VAT for hospitality food services::Measures to improve cost competitiveness and avoid any further cost increases::For a review to be carried out of options for assisting hotels to develop employer-led staff accommodation across the sector::Targeted funding for a national hotel retrofitting scheme to reduce carbon footprint::Increased investment and support for tourism marketing both domestically and overseas.::Insurance reform and policy to avoid any additional increases in insurance costs::Increased investment in training and skills development within the sector::Robust enforcement of regulations around short term lettings::Additional resources for tourism economic research::Avoid the introduction of a tourist tax::Increased air access including removal of cap at Dublin airport

records.specific_details: TENI advocates for equal rights and access to healthcare for trans people.
records.intended_results: We wanted to arrange an AV Room briefing to highlight gaps in access to the Gender Recognition Act
lobbying_activities: 17th July - exchanged text messages with Padraig Rice TD to arrange an AV Room briefing in September - Informal communication

records.specific_details: TENI advocates for equal rights and access to healthcare for trans people.
records.intended_results: We intended to inform DPOs of the current crisis in gender affirming care to better equip them in future planning on new models of care. ::We intended to inform Minister Carroll MacNeil on other issues regarding LGBTQI+ Healthcare, including mental health and elderly care.
lobbying_activities: 12th May - text messages exchanged with Padraig Rice on healthcare questions for Minister for Health - Informal communication

16th May - Meeting with Minister for Health Jennifer Carroll MacNeil on LGBTQI+ health issues. These issues included gender affirming care, lgbtqia+ mental health, and elderly care. Follow up meetings were also planned in this meeting, though have not taken place as of the end of this lobbying log period - Meeting

1st May - Meeting with Mary Lou McDonald and David Cullinane, seeking clarity on SF comments regarding the UK Supreme Court decision on gender, updates on transgender healthcare both Ireland and Northern Ireland, and wider LGBTQ issues - Meeting

24th June - education session on trans healthcare and trans rights for Sinn Fein. Elected representatives present were Máire Devine TD, Chris Andrews TD, and from Dublin City Council: Kourtney Kenny, John Smith, Willo Carey, Niamh Whelan. - Meeting
29th July - Sinéad Gibney TD emailed to invite TENI to speak on a panel in November - Email

2nd August - Met the Minister for Mental Health, Mary Butler at All Together Now we talked about healthcare for under 16s and trans healthcare in general. She updated us on conversations she was having. - Event / Reception
2nd July - education session on trans healthcare with Mark Wall TD and Duncan Smith TD - Meeting

records.specific_details: The Wheel hosted launch event for our Pre-Budget Submission 2026. It comprised a panel discussion with speakers from community and voluntary organisations and an overview of The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026. We invited Ministers, TDs and Senators from the most relevant departments and joint Oireachtas committees and several attended in person.
records.intended_results: To inform TDs and Senators about The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026, which includes issues such as sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.

records.lobbyist_name: KARE Promoting People with Intellectual Disabilities
records.specific_details: Kare (Promoting  Inclusion for People with Intellectual Disabilities) are members of the umbrella organisation National Federation of Voluntary Service Providers (NFVSP) which represents over 53 organisations nationally. Kare participated in a campaign organised by the NFVSP on the General Election 2026 to highlight issues facing people with intellectual disability and their families.
records.intended_results: To welcome an opportunity to meet representatives to highlight some of the many issues being experienced by people with intellectual disabilities and their families along with the key disability issues directly impacting people living in the different constituency areas.
lobbying_activities: 17 letters were emailed to DPOs across Offaly, Wicklow, North / South Kildare constituencies by email extending an invite to meet to with them to highlight the many issues being experienced by people with intellectual disabilities and their families in their constituencies. Accompanying this letter was a one page outlining the NFVSP key priorities for budget 2026. - Letter

One virtual meeting took place on the 28th July with TD Martin Heydon, Minister for Agriculture, Food and the Marine (Kildare South - Fine Gael) between Kare & Muiríosa to discuss the NFVSP supporting people with intellectual disabilities key priorities for Budget 2026. - Virtual Meeting

One virtual meeting took place with TD Seán Ó Fearghaíl (Kildare South - Fianna Fáil) the 24th July to discuss the NFVSP supporting people with intellectual disabilities key priorities for Budget 2026. - Virtual Meeting

One virtual meeting took place with TD Shónagh Ní Raghallaigh (Kildare South - Sinn Féin) on the 31st July to discuss the NFVSP supporting people with intellectual disabilities key priorities for Budget 2026. - Virtual Meeting

records.intended_results: To secure support from members of the Oireachtas for the work of the Northern Ireland Assembly All-Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, in particular to help draw attention to gambling marketing in sport in Ireland and the UK.
lobbying_activities: On [1 July] met with Joanna Byrne TD and Senator Chris Andrews to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Meeting

On [17 June, 25 June] emailed Mark Wall TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Meeting

On [30 June] spoke with the office of Emer Currie TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Phone call
On [7 August] emailed Erin McGreehan TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [7 August] emailed Senator Evanne Ní Chuilinn seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [9 July, 31 July, 20 August] emailed Joanna Byrne TD to invite her to attend a 5-a-side tournament at Stormont to raise awareness of gambling advertising in sport. Asked for her assistance in putting together an Oireachtas team to participate. - Email

records.specific_details: Emails to Members of the Oireachtas Committee on Social Protection, Community & Local Development requesting to put combatting poverty at the top of the committees agenda, with particular reference to income adequacy, accessible public services & measures targeted at the most disadvantaged groups
records.intended_results: To influence decision makers to be more conscious of the impact of poverty on the individuals affected and society as a whole by providing information and research in relation to legislation and policy in this area with particular regard to the area of income adequacy, availability of public services, and targeted measures for the most disadvantaged gorups
lobbying_activities: Email contact with Chair & Members of the Committee for Social Protection, Community & Rural Development requesting contact in relation to the work of the committee in pre-budget preparation, the need for income adequacy, the new Anti-Poverty strategy and the need to add socio-economic disadvantage as a ground for discrimination in Equality legislation - Email

Email request to Liam Quaide TD & Mark Wall TD to submit Parliamentary Questions in relation to income adequacy, the need for socio-economic status to be included as a ground for discrimination under the equality act. - Email
Email to Luke Ming Flanagan MEP & Michael McNamara requesting a visit to the European Parliament - Email

Email to the chair and individual members of the Committee on Social Protection who had responded to our letter of introduction, regarding placing the Anti-poverty Strategy on the work programme of the committee for the autumn Liam Quaide, Mark Wall, Pat the Cope Gallagher & Catherine Ardagh - Email

Follow on meeting with Liam Quaide TD and the assistant of Mark Wall TD in Leinster House 17/6 in relation to their work as Social Protection Spokespersons for their parties and the work of the committee - Meeting
Submission of Amendments to Aodhan O'Riordan MEP as part of the Eruopean Parliament's Proposals for the EU Anti-poverty Strategy - Email

Submission to the committee on social protection pre-budget scrutiny. Submission to the joint committee on COM(2025) 123 Proposal for a regulation of the European Parliament and of the Council amending EU Regulations (EU) 2021/1058 and EU 2021/1056 as regards specific measures to address strategic measures as part of mid term review - Submission

records.lobbyist_name: National Forum of Family Resource Centres CLG
records.specific_details: The Family Resource Centre National Forum held a political engagement event on the 28th of May in Dublin.
records.intended_results: Raise profile of the National FRC Programmeand provided an opportunity for Oireachtasmembers to meet with FRC representativesfrom their region.Launch of the FRC National Forum Pre-Budget Submission for 2026 includingseeking an optimum core funding of €240,000for all 121 Family Resource Centres, a capital funding stream for FRCs and funding for counselling services.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Further and Higher Education, Research, Innovation and Science

lobbying_activities: Political engagement event held on the 28th of May in Buswellshotel in Dublin. Included launch of the FRCNF Pre-Budget Submissionfor 2026. - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Sent Pre-Budget Submission to the Joint Oireachtas Committee on Social Protection. This Submission was invited by the Committee as part of the annual Pre-Budget process. We have registered this lobbying with each member of the Committee out of an abundance of caution.
records.intended_results: i) Publish a timeline for implementation of the Programme for Government Actionto abolish the means test for Carers Allowance within the lifetime of thisGovernment.  ::i) Increase the weekly limit for education, work and voluntary hours for those inreceipt of Carers Allowance, Carers Benefit, and Carer Support Grant. Werecommend a minimum of 20 hours per week.ii) Examine the current methods for calculation of the permissible working hoursto take account of once-off, seasonal work available to family carers. ::i) Raise the levels of payments for Carers Allowanceii) Support the recent ask from the Oireachtas Disability Group, calling for a Costof Disability payment of €55 per week (€2, 860 per annum)iii) Raise the level of the Carer Support Grant to €2,060, in line with the projectedaverage wage increase in 2026.
lobbying_activities: Email

records.specific_details: Active Retirement Ireland's policy recommendations as included in the pre-budget submission to the Department of Social Protection. Policy recommendations pertain to pension reform, income supports, Treatment Benefit Scheme (specifically inclusion of hearing tests), as well as whole of government strategies for positive aging, combatting ageism and the establishment of an Independent Commissioner for Ageing and Older People.
records.intended_results: Consideration of/ action around policy recommendations raised to better support older people in Ireland.
lobbying_activities: Active Retirement Ireland Policy and Programmes Manager Alison Bough attended the Department of Social Protection Pre-Budget Forum to further support and discuss Active Retirement Ireland's submitted policy recommendations. - Event / Reception

Active Retirement Ireland's Pre-Budget 2026 Submission to the Department of Social Protection was sent by email to the Department, to relevant government ministers and to opposition spokespeople. - Email

records.specific_details: National One Parent Family Association Pre-budget submission Briefing
records.intended_results: To highlight NOPFA's pre-budgetary recommendations in order to increase resources directed to assist lone parents/one parent families at risk of homelessness::Increase resources directed to assist lone parents/one parent families at risk of homelessness
lobbying_activities: One public event on the 15th of July in Buswell's Hotel, Dublin.  Attended as a member of NOPFA at SVP pre budget submission. - Event / Reception

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: We contacted Deputies Wall, Nash, Kenny, Sherlock, and Senator Harmon, in their respective capacities as Labour's disability,  social protection, education, and health spokespeople, to invite them to a briefing of Inclusion Ireland's Pre-Budget Submission's priorities, along with a fully copy of the document for their consideration.
records.intended_results: Inclusion Ireland sought to influence Labour's proposals around disability matters ahead of their own Pre-Budget Submission.
lobbying_activities: Deputies Nash and Sherlock's parliamentary assistants separately replied to acknowledge their receiving our submission and that they would bring them to the Deputies' attention. - Email
